input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known issue/change in .NET MAUI.

Let me provide guidance based on what I know about this topic.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated and removed in newer versions. The recommended approach for .NET 10 (and starting from .NET 9 in some cases) is different.


Actually, I need to reconsider this. Android deprecated `adjustResize` back in Android 11 with the introduction of edge-to-edge layouts, and .NET MAUI's handling of soft input modes has shifted accordingly. The better approach now is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or work with edge-to-edge considerations rather than relying on the older `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` method. So with .NET 9+, edge-to-edge mode changed how keyboard handling works on Android—`adjustResize` no longer functions the same way, and you need to manually listen to window insets from the IME instead. The simplest solution is wrapping content in a `ScrollView` to let it handle the keyboard appearance automatically. I can set up a behavior or custom handler that listens to window insets and adjusts the bottom padding dynamically when the keyboard appears, which is the modern approach for handling IME in edge-to-edge mode. Alternatively, I could disable edge-to-edge entirely to restore the old `adjustResize` behavior, though that's not ideal long-term.
